title: The Monkey King is Born
overview: A stone monkey hatches from a rock on Flower Fruit Mountain and becomes king after successfully leaping behind a waterfall.

title: Obtaining Treasure from the Dragon Palace
overview: Seeking a suitable weapon, the Monkey King creates a disturbance in the Dragon King's underwater palace and obtains the Ruyi Jingu Bang (Golden-Hooped Rod).

title: Trouble While Tending Horses
overview: Sun Wukong is offered a minor position in Heaven as Keeper of the Heavenly Horses (Bimawen) but, feeling insulted by the title, he rebels.

title: Havoc in Heaven's Palace
overview: Having declared himself "Great Sage, Equal of Heaven," Wukong fights the Celestial Army and wreaks havoc in the main halls of Heaven.

title: Great Battle with Erlang Shen
overview: The Jade Emperor summons his nephew, the powerful Erlang Shen, who battles Wukong in a fierce duel and finally captures him.

title: Cannot Escape Buddha's Palm (1)
overview: Wukong is trapped in Laozi’s furnace, but he escapes; the Buddha intervenes, challenging Wukong to leap out of his palm.

title: Cannot Escape Buddha's Palm (2)
overview: Wukong attempts the impossible leap but fails and is imprisoned by the Buddha beneath the massive Five-Elements Mountain.

title: Happy Reunion of Master and Disciple
overview: The Tang Monk, Xuanzang (later Tripitaka), begins his journey to the West and passes the mountain, freeing Sun Wukong to be his disciple.

title: Cleverly Subduing the White Dragon Horse
overview: A dragon, son of the Dragon King, eats Xuanzang’s horse; Wukong helps subdue the dragon, who is then transformed into Xuanzang's new white steed.

title: The Evil Monk Steals the Kasaya
overview: The pilgrims stay at a monastery where a greedy old abbot plots to steal the precious Kasaya (monk's robe) given to Xuanzang by Guanyin.

title: Subduing the Black Bear Demon
overview: The abbot’s black bear demon friend steals the robe; Wukong battles and subdues the demon, recovering the garment.

title: Pigsy Becomes a Disciple
overview: In the Gaolao Village, Wukong subdues the lustful pig demon, Zhu Bajie (Pigsy), who agrees to join the pilgrimage as a second disciple.

title: Dangerously Passing Yellow Wind Ridge
overview: The group encounters a demon on Yellow Wind Ridge who uses a powerful sandstorm, forcing Wukong to seek aid from a divine helper.

title: Accepting a Disciple at the Quicksand River
overview: At the treacherous Quicksand River, the last disciple, Sha Wujing (Sandy), is subdued and joins the group.

title: Stealing and Eating the Ginseng Fruit
overview: At the Wuzhuang Temple, Pigsy and Wukong steal the temple’s precious Ginseng Fruits, which are said to grant immortality.

title: Guanyin Saves the Immortal Tree
overview: The angry Taoist master, Zhenyuan Great Immortal, catches the disciples; Wukong damages the immortal tree, which Guanyin must heal to resolve the conflict.

title: Three Attacks on the White Bone Demon
overview: A female demon tries three times to capture Xuanzang by transforming into a woman, an old lady, and an old man, but Wukong sees through her disguises.

title: Mistakenly Entering the Wave Moon Cave
overview: Xuanzang, furious after Wukong seemingly kills innocent people (the demon's disguises), dismisses Wukong from the pilgrimage.

title: Tang Sanzang Turns into a Tiger
overview: Xuanzang is captured by the Yellow Robe Demon in the Wave Moon Cave, who transforms the monk into a tiger.

title: Pigsy Invites Wukong
overview: Pigsy is sent to Flower Fruit Mountain to beg Wukong to return and save their Master from the demon.

title: Danger at Flat-Top Mountain
overview: The pilgrims encounter the Gold and Silver Horned Kings, two powerful demons armed with magical calabash gourds and ropes.

title: Subduing the Demon at Lotus Cave
overview: Wukong uses his cleverness and transformations to trick and capture the two demon kings, recovering the powerful magical items.

title: The Old King Entrusts in a Dream
overview: In the Kingdom of Wuji, Xuanzang receives a dream from the deceased old king, revealing that a fake king (a demon) has taken his place.

title: Eliminating the Demon in the Kingdom of Wuji
overview: Wukong finds the old king's body in a well and helps restore him, then battles and defeats the shapeshifting demon that had usurped the throne.

title: Wukong Meets the Holy Child
overview: The group encounters the Red Boy (Hong Hai'er), the son of the Bull Demon King, a powerful demon child who captures Xuanzang.

title: Subduing Red Boy
overview: Red Boy's powerful Samadhi True Fire nearly defeats Wukong, who must appeal to Guanyin to help subdue the child, who becomes her disciple.

title: Contest of Strength in the Chechi Kingdom
overview: In the Chechi Kingdom, the group finds three Daoist Immortals oppressing Buddhist monks; Wukong challenges them to a contest of magic.

title: Great Sage Eliminates Three Demons
overview: Wukong, Bajie, and Sandy defeat the three demon immortals—a Tiger, a Deer, and an Antelope spirit—in a battle of supernatural skills.

title: Night Obstruction at the Heaven-Connecting River
overview: The pilgrims are stopped by the vast Heaven-Connecting River, which they can't cross, due to the influence of the Great White Turtle and a demon.

title: Hard Fight with the One-Horned Monster
overview: The group encounters a powerful water demon known as the Lingmo, or "One-Horned Monster," who captures the Master.

title: Strange Encounter in the Women's Kingdom
overview: The pilgrims enter the Kingdom of Women, where Xuanzang is pressured to marry the queen, but Wukong devises a trick to escape.

title: Forced Marriage at Pipa Cave
overview: After leaving the kingdom, Xuanzang is captured by the Scorpion Demoness in the Pipa Cave, who tries to force him into marriage.

title: The Real and Fake Sun Wukong
overview: A Six-Eared Macaque appears, impersonating Wukong perfectly and causing trouble; the two look-alikes fight bitterly.

title: Buddha Recognizes the Macaque
overview: The two Wukongs go before the Buddha, who uses his spiritual sight to identify and expose the true identity of the Six-Eared Macaque, who is then slain.

title: Trapped in the Flaming Mountains
overview: The journey is halted by the immense Flaming Mountains, which are too hot to cross, and they require the magical Iron Fan.

title: Fierce Battle with the Bull Demon King
overview: Wukong fights his sworn brother, the Bull Demon King, to obtain the Iron Fan, which is held by his wife, Princess Iron Fan.

title: Burning the Silk-Web Cave
overview: Pigsy is captured by seven beautiful Spider Demons who try to lure him; Wukong attempts to rescue him and burns their lair.

title: Eliminating the Centipede Demon
overview: The Master is poisoned by the Centipede Demon, the sworn brother of the Spider Demons; Wukong defeats the demon with help from a divine Star Lord.

title: Redressing the Wrong at the Golden Light Pagoda
overview: Wukong investigates the theft of the Golden Light Treasure Pagoda relics by two demons, resolving a false accusation against two priests.

title: Suffering Misfortune in the Fake Western Heaven
overview: The pilgrims encounter a group of demons disguised as the Buddha and his disciples, who capture Xuanzang in a false monastery.

title: Maitreya Subdues the Demon Boy
overview: Wukong discovers the mastermind is the demon boy Huang Mei, who stole the magic of the Maitreya Buddha, who must come to subdue his own boy servant.

title: Wukong Becomes a Famous Doctor
overview: In the Kingdom of India, Wukong acts as a traveling physician to cure the Queen, whose ailment is caused by a demon.

title: Cleverly Obtaining the Purple-Gold Bell
overview: The Master is captured by a powerful Lion Demon armed with a magical Purple-Gold Bell that causes a lot of trouble for Wukong.

title: Bravely Charging Lion Camel Ridge
overview: The group faces the immense Lion Camel Ridge, where three formidable demon kings (Lion, Elephant, and Roc) hold an entire city captive.

title: Great Sage Battles Three Demons
overview: Wukong fights the three powerful demon kings, who are finally revealed to be mounts of Manjusri, Samantabhadra, and the Buddha himself, who subdues them.

title: Rescuing the Kingdom of Biqu
overview: The group encounters a king who is being advised by a deer demon disguised as a Daoist, who is secretly stealing the life essence of children.

title: Filling the Bottomless Pit
overview: The Rat Demoness of the Bottomless Pit captures the Master; Wukong seeks help from Heaven, as the demon is the mouse who ate the Buddha's oil.

title: The Confused Old King
overview: The king of the Kingdom of Xuanyang is confused and Wukong must clarify the situation surrounding the capture of Xuanzang.

title: Causing Havoc in the Linked Caves
overview: The Master is captured by the Rhinoceros King, who has linked his cave to the palace, causing further chaos before he is subdued.

title: Seeking a Husband in the India Kingdom
overview: The group arrives in the India Kingdom, where the princess announces she is seeking a husband; Wukong suspects something is wrong.

title: Chang'e Subdues the Jade Rabbit
overview: Wukong discovers that the princess is actually the Jade Rabbit from the moon (servant of the Moon Goddess, Chang'e), who captured the real princess.

title: Obtaining the True Scriptures at Vulture Peak
overview: After enduring eighty-one trials, the pilgrims finally reach Vulture Peak (Griddhrakuta) in the West and are granted the sacred scriptures by the Buddha.
